title: Lucero House, Floor Plan and Roof/Vega Plan (HABS NM-129, sheet 2)
era_or_year: vernacular adobe (19th century); HABS drawings documented after 1933
creator: Historic American Buildings Survey
research_notes: Floor plan is sheet 2 (00002v.jpg) of HABS NM-129 'Drawings'. Combined sheet: floor plan + vega/roof plan + fireplace section/elevation. Thick masonry walls make a good distinct-wall-thickness test case.
research_features: Adobe dwelling with very thick (hatched) adobe walls, labeled rooms (Bedroom, Kitchen, Zaguan covered passage), and a later bedroom addition. Combined sheet also includes a roof vega (ceiling beam) plan and fireplace details.
feature_highlights: very thick adobe walls drawn with dense diagonal poché hatching
ZAGUAN covered through-passage running between the rooms
four labeled rooms (two Bedrooms, Kitchen, Bedroom) around a central spine
later bedroom addition flagged with lighter wall hatching
combined sheet: floor plan plus a vega (ceiling-beam) roof plan
corner adobe fireplace details (section, elevation, plan)
circled keys tying openings and beams to schedules
supported_examples: straight adobe walls with substantial thickness and junctions
raster underlay calibration from the floor-plan portion of the sheet
roadmap_examples: wall construction profiles and poché for thick adobe masonry
furniture/fixture placement for the corner adobe fireplaces
site/textual metadata for the addition note

gap_features.why: the ZAGUAN is a roofed covered passage cut through the house that must be drawable as an open space, not a fully enclosed room
